summary: Verified the dual-voltage heater spec content edit across /app/frontend/index.html, build-static.js, and all pre-rendered pages. Created /app/backend/tests/test_heater_spec_updates.py with 29 tests covering all 5 placement variants (spec tables, prose meta descriptions, JSON-LD Product Heater property, hero trust-pill + spec-chips, feature cards + new .feature-subcopy CSS class) plus regressions (offers block, sitemap, _redirects, _headers, pre-rendered pages) plus a live preview URL smoke test. All 29 new tests pass. Full suite regression: 153/153 pass. No issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: index.html is very large (>3000 lines) and mixes CSS, JS templating, and HTML for 6 routes. Long-term this should be split; not blocking for this content-edit sweep.

The 'cr1'/'cr2'/'cr3' key is reused in three separate JS objects (slug map, title map, description map). A regex like /'cr1':\s*'...'/ matches the wrong one; keys should be namespaced (e.g. cr1Title, cr1Desc) or wrapped in a single ROUTES object to avoid ambiguity.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Content edit sweep for '3 kW Heater' → dual-voltage. All 29 heater-spec assertions pass: (1) zero OLD-string occurrences across index.html, build-static.js, and 6 pre-rendered files; (2) 3 long-form spec-table Heater rows in master; (3) 3 comparison-table rows (with col-current cell per active column); (4) 1 hero trust-pill + 3 spec-chips = 4 total CHIP_STR occurrences; (5) 2 Dual-Voltage feature-cards (CR1+CR3) with 2 .feature-subcopy divs; (6) .feature-subcopy CSS class has font-size + color/opacity; (7) CR1 prose description in dynamic descriptions object updated (longest of 3 'cr1' matches); (8) build-static.js CR1 description + 3 Heater properties updated; (9) JSON-LD Product.additionalProperty.Heater='1 kW (120V) / 4 kW (240V)' on all 3 product pages; (10) regressions intact — offers block (1 each), sitemap 6 urls, _redirects 20 rules, _headers 15 blocks; (11) Preview URL /cr1 serves updated content. Full pytest suite (153 tests) still green.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bug. Content edit verified end-to-end.
